48:38 EFFORTS AIMED AT REDUCING GANG

48:50 IN LAW ENFORCEMENT GRANT.

48:54 COMPREHENSIVE PUBLIC SAFETY

48:56 STRATEGY AND HAS FUNDED

48:59 APPROXIMATELY 250 NONPROFIT

49:09 ORGANIZATIONS.

54:01 PLANNING DEVELOPMENT ON

54:03 TRANSPORTATION TO WHICH WAS

54:04 REFERRED TO ON MARCH 13TH, 2026

54:07 MESSAGE IN ORDER AUTHORIZING

54:09 THE CITY OF BOSTON ACTING

54:10 THROUGH THE MAYOR'S OFFICE

54:12 OF HOUSING TO ACCEPT AND EXPAND

54:15 PAYMENTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $30

54:17 MILLION GIVEN TO THE CITY

54:19 INCLUSIONARY DEVELOPMENT POLICY

54:21 FUND TO CITY OF BOSTON

54:24 INCLUSIONARY DEVELOPMENT POLICY

54:26 FUND WAS ESTABLISHED BY

54:29 EXECUTIVE ORDER IN FEBRUARY

54:31 2000 TO SUPPORT THE PRODUCTION

54:33 AND PREVENTION OF AFFORDABLE

54:35 HOUSING AND NEW MARKET RATE

54:38 HOUSING DEVELOPMENT.

54:38 THE IDP REQUIRES ALL DEVELOPERS

54:42 OF RESIDENTIAL DEVELOPMENT

54:45 PROJECTS WHICH TEN OR MORE

54:46 UNITS TO INCLUDE AFFORDABLE

54:48 UNITS IN THEIR PROJECT AS A

54:51 CONDITION OF RECEIVING RELIEF

54:53 FROM THE BOSTON ZONING CODE FOR

54:55 EITHER THE EXCLUSIVE OR THE

54:58 EXCLUSIVE ORDER PROVIDES THE

54:59 OPTION FOR DEVELOPERS TO MAKE

55:02 CONTRIBUTION TO THE IDP FUND IN

55:04 LIEU OF PROVIDING ON SITE

55:06 AFFORDABLE UNITS SUBJECT TO THE

55:08 APPROVAL OF BOSTON PLANNING AND

55:10 DEVELOPMENT AGENCY SUBMITS A

55:12 REPORT RECOMMENDING THAT THE

55:14 ORDER OUGHT TO PASS.

55:16 THANK YOU.

55:17 CHAIR RECOGNIZES THE CHAIR

55:18 OF THE COMMITTEE ON PLANNING,

55:19 DEVELOPMENT AND TRANSPORTATION.

55:21 COUNCILOR DURKAN, YOU HAVE THE

55:22 FLOOR.

55:22 THANK YOU SO MUCH, CHAIR.

55:23 I THINK YOU JUST GAVE ME A

55:25 NOW I'M THE HEAD OF THE

55:26 BUT DON'T TELL KYRA.

55:29 THANK YOU.

55:30 COUNCIL PRESIDENT.

55:31 ON THURSDAY, JUNE 25TH, THE

55:33 COMMITTEE ON PLANNING

55:33 DEVELOPMENT AND TRANSPORTATION

55:35 CONVENED A HEARING ON DOCKET

55:36 0968, AN ORDER AUTHORIZING THE

55:38 MAYOR'S OFFICE OF HOUSING TO

55:40 ACCEPT AND EXPAND 30 MILLION

55:42 FROM THE INCLUSIONARY

55:43 DEVELOPMENT POLICY FUND.

55:44 I WAS JOINED BY MY COLLEAGUE

55:46 COUNCILOR FLYNN, AND THE

55:47 COMMITTEE HEARD TESTIMONY FROM

55:48 THE MAYOR'S OFFICE OF HOUSING

55:49 OFFICIALS.

55:50 RICK WILSON, THE DIRECTOR

55:51 OF ADMINISTRATION AND FINANCE

55:52 CHRISTINE O'KEEFE, DIRECTOR

55:54 OF NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT AND

55:55 ANDY FELDMAN, HOUSING POLICY

55:57 MANAGER.

55:58 INCLUSIONARY ZONING WAS

55:59 ESTABLISHED BY EXECUTIVE ORDER

56:01 IN FEBRUARY OF 2000 TO SUPPORT

56:03 PRODUCTION AND PRESERVATION

56:04 OF AFFORDABLE HOUSING AND NEW

56:05 MARKET RATE HOUSING IN BOSTON.

56:07 THE OFFICIALS DESCRIBE THE

56:09 EVOLUTION OF IDP POLICY AND

56:10 OVERSIGHT, NOTING THAT THE

56:12 ADMINISTRATION WAS TRANSFERRED

56:14 IT FROM THE BPD IN 2013.

56:16 THEY ALSO DISCUSSED THE 2023

56:19 POLICY UPDATES WHICH INCREASE

56:20 IDP PERCENTAGES.

56:22 THESE FUNDS ARE IDP REVENUE

56:24 GENERATED FROM PAYMENTS MADE IN

56:26 LIEU OF ON SITE OR OFF SITE

56:28 UNITS, AS WELL AS OTHER ONE

56:30 TIME PARTIAL UNIT PAYMENTS

56:31 OVERSEEN BY THE BPA.

56:33 THE PANELISTS SHARED MORE THAN

56:35 200 MILLION OF IDP REVENUE HAS

56:37 BEEN COLLECTED SINCE THE

56:38 POLICIES INCEPTION AND

56:40 PROJECTED THAT THE PROGRAM WILL

56:41 COLLECT 26 MILLION IN FISCAL

56:43 YEAR 27 AND 28 CONSISTENT WITH

56:46 THE TYPICAL ANNUAL RANGE THE 30

56:48 MILLION AUTHORIZED IN THIS

56:50 ORDER, TOGETHER WITH THE 6

56:51 MILLION THAT WAS PREVIOUSLY

56:52 AUTHORIZED HAS BEEN ALLOCATED

56:54 TO TEN AFFORDABLE HOUSING

56:55 PROJECTS INCLUDING BOTH RENTAL

56:56 AND HOMEOWNERSHIP OPPORTUNITIES

56:58 ACROSS ROXBURY, MATTAPAN, EAST

57:00 BOSTON, DORCHESTER AND

57:02 CHINATOWN.

57:02 MANY OF THESE PROJECTS ARE

59:01 SPECIAL LAW REGARDING AN ACT

59:03 RELATIVE TO INCREASING CIVIL

59:05 PENALTIES FOR ILLEGAL

59:07 POSSESSION, USE AND SALE

59:09 OF FIREWORKS IN THE CITY

59:10 OF BOSTON.

59:11 THE CHAIR RECOGNIZES

59:12 COUNCILOR CULPEPER

59:13 COUNCILOR YOU HAVE THE FLOOR.

59:14 MADAM PRESIDENT.

59:15 EVERY YEAR NEIGHBORHOODS ACROSS

59:18 BOSTON EXPERIENCED THE

59:21 CONSEQUENCES OF ILLEGAL

59:22 FIREWORKS.

59:23 RESIDENTS LOSE SLEEP.

59:26 VETERANS AND OTHERS LIVING WITH

59:27 PTSD ARE AFFECTED BY REPEATED

59:29 EXPLOSIONS.

59:31 PETS BECOME DISTRESSED FAMILIES

59:33 WORRIED ABOUT THE RISKS

59:34 OF FIRES, INJURIES AND PROPERTY

59:37 DAMAGE.

59:37 OUR FIRST RESPONDERS ARE

59:39 REGULARLY REGULARLY CALLED UPON

59:42 TO RESPOND TO COMPLAINTS AND

59:44 EMERGENCY STEMMING FROM ILLEGAL

59:46 FIREWORKS, PARTICULARLY IN THE

59:49 SUMMER MONTHS ESPECIALLY DURING

59:53 JULY 4TH.

59:54 THIS HOME PETITION INCREASES

59:56 THE CIVIL FINE FOR THE ILLEGAL

59:59 POSSESSION OR USE OF FIREWORKS

1:00:03 TO BETWEEN 100 AND $1000 AND

1:00:04 INCREASES THE CIVIL FINE FOR

1:00:05 THE ILLEGAL SALE OR OFFERING

1:00:07 FOR SALE OF FIREWORKS TO

1:00:11 BETWEEN 500 AND $5000.

1:00:12 ALL EXISTING ENFORCEMENT

1:00:14 PROVISIONS INCLUDING SEIZURE

1:00:17 FOR FORFEITURE, ARREST

1:00:19 AUTHORITY AND ALL APPLICABLE

1:00:21 CRIMINAL PENALTIES REMAIN

1:00:23 UNCHANGED.

1:00:24 THE GOAL OF THIS PETITION IS

1:00:25 SIMPLE TO PROVIDE A STRONGER

1:00:28 DETERRENT AGAINST CONDUCT THAT

1:00:29 CONTINUES TO THREATEN PUBLIC

1:00:31 SAFETY AND DISRUPT

1:00:33 NEIGHBORHOODS ACROSS OUR CITY.

1:00:35 ILLEGAL FIREWORKS ARE NOT A

1:00:37 HARMLESS NUISANCE THEY CREATE

1:00:39 REAL RISKS FOR RESIDENTS,

1:00:42 FIREFIGHTERS, EMERGENCY

1:00:43 PERSONNEL AND PROPERTY OWNERS

1:00:45 BY UPDATING FINES THAT HAVE NOT

1:00:47 KEPT PACE WITH THE SERIOUSNESS

1:00:49 OF THESE VIOLATIONS.

1:00:50 WE'RE STRENGTHENING A TOOL TO

1:00:52 DISCOURAGE ILLEGAL FIREWORKS

1:00:54 WHILE PRESERVING THE BROAD

1:00:55 ENFORCEMENT FRAMEWORK ALREADY

1:00:57 ESTABLISHED UNDER STATE LAW.

1:00:59 AND I MIGHT ADD, ONE OF THE

1:01:01 REASONS THAT REALLY MOVED ME TO

1:01:04 FILE THIS IS SITTING UP AT

1:01:07 NIGHT GETTING CALLS FROM FOLKS

1:01:10 ABOUT FIREWORKS AND THE

1:01:12 POSSIBILITY THAT A HOME OR HOME

1:01:14 WOULD BE SET ON FIRE.

1:01:16 IT'S AMAZING THAT WE HAVE TO

1:01:18 EVERY YEAR DURING JULY 4TH GO

1:01:21 THROUGH THE SAME THING OVER AND

1:01:22 OVER AND JUST SIT THERE AND NOT

1:01:24 OUR HEADS.

1:01:25 BUT I THINK BEFORE SOMETHING

1:01:27 BECOMES SERIOUSLY LIKE A HOME

1:01:29 CATCHING FIRE THAT THIS

1:01:32 COUNCIL NEED TO ACT.

1:01:35 AND I PUT THIS FORWARD WITH ALL

1:01:37 SERIOUSNESS BECAUSE A 4TH

1:01:40 OF JULY NIGHT FIREWORKS ARE

1:01:42 GOING CRAZY.

1:01:45 SOME YOUTH ABOUT 20 OF THEM AND

1:01:48 ONE OF THE UH PERSONS THERE

1:01:52 HERE AND I'M THE MOTHER OF MANY

1:01:55 OF THESE KIDS.

1:01:58 RESPONSIBLE.

1:02:01 RESPONSIBILITY.

1:02:02 BAG THE NEXT MORNING THEY

1:02:12 NEED TO BE ABLE TO WORK TO, UH

1:02:17 ILLEGAL FIREWORKS AND THE CITY

1:02:19 OF BOSTON.

1:04:11 BY FEDERAL LAW, INDIVIDUALS

1:04:14 WOULD AND STUDENTS AND

1:04:16 INDIVIDUALS WITH DISABILITIES

1:04:19 UM PLAY A CRITICAL ROLE IN OUR

1:04:21 CITY AND OUR COUNTRY.

1:04:25 INDIVIDUALS WITH DISABILITIES

1:04:28 ACT GOVERNS HOW STATES PROVIDE

1:04:30 EARLY INTERVENTION IN SPECIAL

1:04:33 EDUCATION FOR ELIGIBLE STUDENTS

1:04:34 WITH DISABILITIES INCLUDING

1:04:36 INFANTS, TODDLERS AND CHILDREN

1:04:39 IN GRADE SCHOOL AND

1:04:41 MASSACHUSETTS.

1:04:42 ALL STUDENTS ARE ENTITLED TO A

1:04:43 FREE APPROPRIATE PUBLIC

1:04:46 EDUCATION IN THE LEAST

1:04:48 RESTRICTIVE ENVIRONMENT,

1:04:50 MEANING THAT ALL STUDENTS WITH

1:04:52 DISABILITIES ARE PERMITTED TO

1:04:53 SERVICES LIKE SPEECH AND

1:04:55 LANGUAGE THERAPY, OCCUPATIONAL

1:04:57 THERAPY, PHYSICAL THERAPY,

1:05:01 BEHAVIORAL THERAPY, ASSISTIVE

1:05:04 TECHNOLOGY, TRANSPORTATION AND

1:05:07 OTHER ACADEMIC ACCOMMODATIONS

1:05:10 INCLUDING DEADLINE EXTENSIONS.

1:05:14 THESE PERSONNEL CUTS MAY RESULT

1:05:19 IN DEVASTATING IMPACTS FOR

1:05:20 STUDENTS WITH DISABILITIES AND

1:05:22 LET ME REPHRASE THAT THEY WILL

1:05:24 RESULT IN DEVASTATING IMPACT

1:05:26 FOR STUDENTS WITH DISABILITIES

1:05:29 IN THE ABSENCE

1:05:30 OF PARAPROFESSIONALS, ALL

1:05:31 STUDENTS ESPECIALLY THOSE WITH

1:05:33 DISABILITIES MAY FACE

1:05:34 DIFFICULTIES WITH CLASSROOM

1:05:36 SUPPORT, ACCESSING RESOURCES,

1:05:39 ACCOMMODATIONS AND ULTIMATELY

1:05:41 RESULT IN A DECLINE IN ACADEMIC

1:05:43 AND SOCIAL PROGRAMS WITH THE

1:05:47 PROJECTED LOSS OF SEVERAL

1:05:48 HUNDRED EMPLOYEES, IT'S

1:05:51 CRITICAL THAT THE CITY

1:05:53 COUNCIL PROVIDES OVERSIGHT AND

1:05:55 UNDERSTANDS THE CONSEQUENCES

1:05:57 OF OUR ACTIONS FOR STUDENTS,

1:05:59 FAMILIES AND NEIGHBORS.

1:58:39 FLOOR.

1:58:41 THANK YOU, MR CHAIR.

1:58:42 I RISE TODAY IN SUPPORT OF THIS

1:58:43 HEARING ORDER TO DISCUSS THE

1:58:45 URGENT NEED TO REESTABLISH, UH,

1:58:47 A U.S. POSTAL SERVICE LOCATION

1:58:49 IN AUSTIN.

1:58:50 AUSTIN'S POSTAL BRANCH POST

1:58:52 OFFICE BRANCH WAS CLOSED IN

1:58:53 NOVEMBER OF 2019.

1:58:55 UH, WHEN THE BUILDING WAS

1:58:57 LOCATED IN A BUILDING THAT WAS

1:58:59 DEEMED STRUCTURALLY UNSOUND.

1:59:01 UH, FOR THE PAST SEVEN YEARS,

1:59:03 THE NEARLY 331 SO NEARLY 32,000

1:59:07 RESIDENTS OF AUSTIN HAVE BEEN

1:59:09 WITHOUT A NEIGHBORHOOD POST

1:59:10 OFFICE FORCING FAMILIES,

1:59:12 SENIORS, STUDENTS AND SMALL

1:59:13 BUSINESS OWNERS TO RELY ON A

1:59:14 SINGLE LOCATION IN BRIGHTON

1:59:16 CENTER.

1:59:18 IN JUNE 2025, USPS SENT A

1:59:19 LETTER TO MAIL CONFIRMING THE

1:59:21 FINAL DECISION TO REESTABLISH A

1:59:23 POST OFFICE BRANCH IN ALLSTON

1:59:25 AFTER A FULLY NEGOTIATED LEASE

1:59:27 WITH A NEW DEVELOPMENT

1:59:28 CURRENTLY UNDER CONSTRUCTION AT

1:59:30 THE PREVIOUS POST OFFICE SITE.

1:59:32 IN APRIL OF THIS YEAR MY OFFICE

1:59:35 RECEIVED A LETTER FROM USPS

1:59:36 STATING THAT THEY HAD DECIDED

1:59:38 TO TEMPORARILY PAUSE LOOKING

1:59:40 FOR AN ALTERNATIVE FACILITY

1:59:42 WHILE IT FURTHER REVIEWS THE

1:59:43 OPERATIONAL NEEDS IN ALLSTON.

1:59:45 THIS ABRUPT REVERSAL IS AN

1:59:48 UNCONSCIONABLE, UNACCEPTABLE

1:59:50 BROKEN PROMISE AND TO THE

1:59:51 ALLSTON BRIGHTON COMMUNITY.

1:59:53 I'M CALLING FOR A HEARING TODAY

1:59:54 TO ALLOW THE COUNCIL TO HEAR

1:59:56 DIRECTLY FROM USPS THE

1:59:58 DEVELOPER THAT IS THAT HAD

1:59:59 PREVIOUSLY SECURED A COMMITMENT

2:00:01 FROM USPS CITY OFFICIALS

2:00:03 REGARDING THE DELAY AND TO

2:00:05 IDENTIFY NEXT STEPS.

2:00:07 RESTORING A POST OFFICE IN

2:00:08 AUSTIN IS ABOUT EQUITY,

2:00:10 ACCESSIBILITY AND ENSURING

2:00:11 EVERY NEIGHBORHOOD HAS ACCESS

2:00:13 TO THIS ESSENTIAL PUBLIC

2:00:14 SERVICE.

2:00:15 I HOPE THIS HEARING BRING UH

2:00:17 HELPS BRING ALL PARTIES BACK TO

2:00:19 THE TABLE TO SEE IF WE CAN

2:00:21 FINALLY DELIVER ON THE

2:00:22 COMMITMENT THAT WAS MADE TO THE

2:00:23 PEOPLE OF ALLSTON.

2:00:25 I URGE MY COLLEAGUES TO SUPPORT

2:00:26 THIS HEARING AND LOOK FORWARD

2:00:27 TO THE DISCUSSION.

2:00:28 THANK YOU.

2:01:45 BOSTON CONTINUES TO FACE A

2:01:48 HOUSING AFFORDABILITY CRISIS

2:01:50 BUT AFFORDABILITY ALONE IS NOT

2:01:53 ENOUGH IF RESIDENTS WHO

2:01:55 LAWFULLY QUALIFY FOR HOUSING

2:01:56 ARE STILL DENIED EQUAL ACCESS

2:01:59 BECAUSE OF WHO THEY ARE OR HOW

2:02:01 THEY PAY THEIR RENT.

2:02:03 FAIR HOUSING IS A CIVIL RIGHTS

2:02:05 ISSUE AND EVERY RESIDENT SHOULD

2:02:07 HAVE AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY TO

2:02:09 ACCESS SAFE, STABLE HOUSING

2:02:12 REGARDLESS OF THEIR RACE OR

2:02:13 WHETHER THEY RELY ON A HOUSING

2:02:16 VOUCHER.

2:02:16 RESEARCH CONDUCTED BY THE

2:02:18 SUFFOLK UNIVERSITY LAW SCHOOL

2:02:20 HOUSING DISCRIMINATION TESTING

2:02:22 PROGRAM AND THE BOSTON

2:02:24 FOUNDATION FOUND TROUBLING

2:02:26 EVIDENCE THAT DISCRIMINATION

2:02:28 REMAINS WIDESPREAD IN THE

2:02:30 GREATER BOSTON RENTAL MARKET.

2:02:32 THE REPORT FOUND EVIDENCE

2:02:34 OF VOUCHER BASED DISCRIMINATION

2:02:37 IN 86% OF TESTS AND RACE BASED

2:02:40 DISCRIMINATION IN 71% OF TESTS,

2:02:43 WITH WINNERS RECEIVING

2:02:44 DIFFERENT TREATMENT THROUGHOUT

2:02:45 THE HOUSING SEARCH PROCESS FROM

2:02:48 INITIAL INQUIRIES AND APARTMENT

2:02:51 SHOWINGS TO APPLICATIONS AND

2:02:53 FOLLOW UP COMMUNICATION.

2:02:55 THIS HEARING IS AN OPPORTUNITY

2:02:57 TO BETTER UNDERSTAND THE EXTENT

2:02:59 OF HOUSING DISCRIMINATION IN

2:03:01 BOSTON, EVALUATE WHETHER OUR

2:03:02 CURRENT ENFORCEMENT TOOLS ARE

2:03:05 SUFFICIENT AND IDENTIFY

2:03:06 ADDITIONAL STEPS THE CITY CAN

2:03:08 TAKE TO STRENGTHEN FAIR HOUSING

2:03:10 ,EDUCATION, TESTING,

2:03:13 ENFORCEMENT AND COORDINATION

2:03:14 WITH STATE AND LOCAL PARTNERS.

2:03:16 WE ARE SERIOUS ABOUT EXPANDING

2:03:19 ACCESS TO HOUSING AND COMBATING

2:03:22 SEGREGATION AND COMBATING

2:03:25 SEGREGATION.

2:03:26 THEN WE MUST ALSO ENSURE THAT

2:03:28 RESIDENTS WHO HAVE HOUSING

2:03:29 VOUCHERS OR OTHER LAWFUL

2:03:31 SOURCES OF INCOME ARE NOT

2:03:34 UNLAWFULLY DENIED THE

2:03:36 OPPORTUNITY TO LIVE IN THE

2:03:37 NEIGHBORHOODS OF THE CHOOSING.

2:03:52 UH, WE KNOW RESIDENTS ARE STILL

2:03:54 FACING HOUSING DISCRIMINATION

2:03:56 IN OUR CITY AND IT'S OUR

2:03:57 OBLIGATION AS A CITY COUNCIL TO

2:03:59 MAKE COMBATING THAT

2:04:01 DISCRIMINATION A PRIORITY IN

2:04:02 OUR ACTIONS, NOT JUST IN OUR

2:04:04 VALUE STATEMENTS.

2:04:06 THAT'S EXACTLY WHY WE FILED A

2:04:07 HEARING ORDER EARLIER THIS YEAR

2:04:09 TO DISCUSS THE CITY'S

2:04:10 IMPLEMENTATION OF FAIR HOUSING

2:04:12 PRACTICES AND HOW THE

2:04:13 ADMINISTRATION IS FAIR IN

2:04:15 ACHIEVING THE GOALS LAID OUT IN

2:04:16 THE ORIGINAL AFFIRMATIVELY

2:04:18 FURTHERING FAIR HOUSING

2:04:19 ASSESSMENT.

2:04:20 THAT ASSESSMENT WAS PUT

2:04:21 TOGETHER IN 2016 BY A DEDICATED

2:04:23 TEAM OF HOUSING EXPERTS AND

2:04:25 ADVOCATES CALLED THE COMMUNITY

2:04:27 ADVISORY COMMITTEE TO OUTLINE

2:04:29 HOW THE CITY MUST AFFIRMATIVELY

2:04:30 FURTHER FAIR HOUSING

2:04:31 OPPORTUNITIES FOR ALL OUR

2:04:32 RESIDENTS.

2:04:33 THAT ASSESSMENT SHEDS LIGHT ON

2:04:35 DISCRIMINATION IN OUR CITY WITH

2:04:37 STEPS OUTLINED TO RECTIFY THE

2:04:39 PROBLEM.

2:04:40 IN 2020, BOSTON BECAME THE

2:04:41 FIRST MAJOR CITY IN THE NATION

2:04:44 TO INCLUDE FAIR HOUSING

2:04:45 REQUIREMENTS IN THE ZONING

2:04:46 CODE THAT MANDATE DEVELOPERS TO

2:04:48 TAKE SIGNIFICANT STEPS TO

2:04:49 PREVENT DISPLACEMENT AND ENSURE

2:04:51 EQUITABLE HOUSING ACCESS.

2:04:53 AS A COUNCIL LAST YEAR WE VOTED

2:04:55 11 NO IN FAVOR OF STRENGTHENING

2:04:57 AND PROTECTING OUR FAMILY

2:04:59 FURTHER IN FAIR HOUSING IDEALS.

2:06:42 ACCESS TO RELIABLE, HIGH SPEED

2:06:45 ,HIGH CAPACITY PUBLIC

2:06:47 TRANSPORTATION IS FUNDAMENTALLY

2:06:49 A CIVIL RIGHTS ISSUE BECAUSE IT

2:06:51 DETERMINES WHETHER RESIDENTS

2:06:53 HAVE EQUAL ACCESS TO

2:06:55 EMPLOYMENT, EDUCATION, HEALTH

2:06:58 CARE, CHILD CARE AND ECONOMIC

2:07:02 OPPORTUNITY WHEN SOME NEIGHBORS

2:07:04 RECEIVE SUSTAINED INVESTMENT IN

2:07:06 RAPID TRANSIT WHILE OTHERS WAIT

2:07:08 DECADES FOR PROMISE

2:07:10 IMPROVEMENTS, WE HAVE A

2:07:11 RESPONSIBILITY TO ASK WHETHER

2:07:13 THOSE DECISIONS HAVE PRODUCED

2:07:15 DISPARATE IMPACTS OR WHETHER

2:07:17 EVERY COMMUNITY HAS BEEN

2:07:19 AFFORDED EQUAL OPPORTUNITY.

2:07:21 NEARLY 40 YEARS AGO WHEN THE

2:07:23 ORANGE LINE WAS RELOCATED TO

2:07:25 THE SOUTHWEST CORRIDOR,

2:07:27 RESIDENTS LAUNCHED RAPID

2:07:29 TRANSIT SERVICE ALONG

2:07:30 WASHINGTON STREET WITH THE

2:07:31 UNDERSTANDING THAT COMPARABLE

2:07:32 RAIL SERVICE WOULD REPLACE IT.

2:07:35 THAT PROMISE, MADAM PRESIDENT

2:07:37 AND MY COLLEAGUES, HAS NEVER

2:07:39 BEEN FULLY REALIZED AND TODAY

2:07:41 THE CORRIDOR REMAINS ONE OF THE

2:07:42 BUSIEST BUS CORRIDORS IN THE

2:07:45 COMMONWEALTH WHILE CONTINUING

2:07:47 TO LACK THE PERMANENT RAPID

2:07:49 TRANSIT INFRASTRUCTURE FOUND

2:07:51 ELSEWHERE IN THE CITY.

2:07:53 DURING THAT SAME PERIOD, THE

2:07:55 COMMONWEALTH CONTINUED

2:07:56 INVESTING IN MAJOR RAIL

2:07:58 PROJECTS ELSEWHERE, INCLUDING

2:08:00 THE COMPLETION OF THE RED LINE

2:08:01 EXTENSION TO ALEWIFE IN 1985.

2:08:04 THE RELOCATION OF THE ORANGE

2:08:06 LINE AND CONSTRUCTION OF NEW

2:08:07 SOUTHWEST CORRIDOR STATIONS IN

2:08:10 1987, THE OPENING OF THE SILVER

2:08:12 LINE WATERFRONT IN 2004, THE

2:08:15 OPENING OF ASSEMBLY STATION ON

2:08:17 THE ORANGE LINE IN 2014 WHEN

2:08:19 THE COMPLETION OF THE GREEN

2:08:20 LINE EXTENSION TO SOMERVILLE

2:08:22 AND MEDFORD BEGINNING IN 2022

2:08:25 WELL EACH OF THESE PROJECTS

2:08:27 SERVED IMPORTANT TRANSPORTATION

2:08:29 NEEDS.

2:08:29 THEY ALSO ILLUSTRATE THAT

2:08:31 TRANSFORMATIONAL RAIL

2:08:32 INVESTMENTS HAVE CONTINUED TO

2:08:34 MOVE FORWARD IN OTHER PARTS

2:08:35 OF GREATER BOSTON, WHILE

2:08:37 COMMUNITIES ALONG BLUE HILL

2:08:39 AVENUE HAVE CONTINUED WAITING

2:08:40 FOR THE RAPID TRANSIT

2:08:42 IMPROVEMENTS THEY WERE

2:08:43 PROMISED.

2:08:44 THIS HEARING IS NOT ABOUT THE

2:08:46 MISSION DIMINISHING INVESTMENTS

2:08:47 MADE ELSEWHERE.

2:08:49 IT IS ABOUT ASKING WHETHER THE

2:08:51 DISTRIBUTION OF RAPID TRANSIT

2:08:53 INFRASTRUCTURE OVER THE PAST

2:08:54 FOUR DECADES HAS BEEN EQUITABLE

2:08:56 .

2:08:58 IT IS ABOUT ASKING WHETHER THE

2:09:00 DISTRIBUTION OF RAPID TRANSFER

2:09:02 AND FOR STRUCTURE OVER THE PAST

2:09:05 FOUR DECADES HAS BEEN EQUITABLE

2:09:06 .

2:09:09 IT IS ABOUT ASKING WHETHER THE

2:09:12 DISTRIBUTION OF RAPID TRANSIT

2:09:13 SED INFRASTRUCTURE OVER THE

2:09:15 PAST FOUR DECADES HAS BEEN

2:09:17 EQUITABLE.

2:09:17 WHETHER HISTORIC PLANNING

2:09:19 DECISIONS HAVE HAD A DISPARATE

2:09:21 IMPACT ON PROTECTED COMMUNITIES

2:09:24 AND WHAT STEPS ARE NECESSARY TO

2:09:25 ENSURE THAT FUTURE

2:09:26 TRANSPORTATION INVESTMENTS

2:09:28 ADVANCE EQUAL OPPORTUNITY

2:09:30 RATHER THAN REINFORCE

2:09:32 LONGSTANDING DISPARITIES.

2:50:32 SENATE BILL 3112 WHICH WOULD

2:50:35 EXTEND THE PILOT PROGRAM FOR

2:50:37 MUNICIPALITIES TO TO OPT IN TO

2:50:40 EXTENDING LAST CALL AND

2:50:42 ESTABLISHING SOCIAL DISTRICTS

2:50:43 THROUGH THE REST OF THE SUMMER.

2:50:45 MAYOR WU ADOPTED BOSTON UP TO

2:50:48 BOSTON INTO THE INITIAL PILOT

2:50:50 EARLY ON.

2:50:51 MORE THAN 200 ESTABLISHMENTS

2:50:53 NOW PARTICIPATE AND TWO SOCIAL

2:50:55 DISTRICTS OPERATE ON TIME.

2:50:57 THERE HAVE BEEN THERE HAS BEEN

2:50:59 A POSITIVE RECEPTION.

2:51:01 RESIDENTS AND VISITORS HAVE

2:51:02 EMBRACED THE EXTENDED HOURS AND

2:51:04 SOCIAL DISTRICTS.

2:51:13 STARTED BUT THE PILOT PROGRAM

2:51:15 IS SET TO EXPIRE AT THE END

2:51:16 OF THIS MONTH.

2:51:22 LEGISLATION ON BEACON HILL TO

2:51:25 CITIES AND TOWNS TO CONTINUE

2:51:26 THE PROGRAM THROUGH AUGUST.

2:51:29 MONTHS FOR TOURISM IN BOSTON

2:51:31 AND GIVEN THE STRUGGLES OF THE

2:51:33 HOSPITALITY INDUSTRY THAT FACE

2:51:35 FACED POST-COVID WITH INFLATION

2:51:38 AND LABOR SHORTAGES ALLOWING

2:51:40 BOSTON TO CONTINUE WITH THIS

2:51:41 PILOT PROGRAM AND CREATE MORE

2:51:43 OPPORTUNITIES FOR SMALL

3:03:11 RESIDENTIAL COORDINATORS AT MAB

3:03:16 MORE THAN 100 YEARS, MAB UH

3:03:17 COMMUNITY SERVICES EMPOWERED

3:03:19 INDIVIDUALS WITH DISABILITIES

3:03:21 BY PROVIDING PROGRAMS THAT

3:03:22 PROMOTE INDEPENDENCE, DIGNITY

3:03:23 AND COMMUNITY INCLUSION

3:03:26 THROUGHOUT BOSTON AND THE

3:03:27 COMMONWEALTH.

3:03:27 THE STAFF AT MAB PERFORM

3:03:29 ESSENTIAL WORK EVERY DAY AND

3:03:31 HELP INDIVIDUALS LIVE AS

3:03:33 INDEPENDENTLY AS POSSIBLE.

3:03:34 THESE ARE DEMANDING

3:03:36 RESPONSIBILITIES THAT REQUIRE

3:03:37 PATIENCE, COMPASSION,

3:03:39 PROFESSIONALISM AND DEDICATION

3:03:40 .

3:03:40 YET MANY OF THESE WORKERS

3:03:42 REMAIN UNDERPAID WITH FEW

3:03:43 BENEFITS.

3:03:47 SEIU 509 THESE EMPLOYEES HAVE

3:03:50 BEEN WORKING TOWARDS A FIRST

3:03:52 CONTRACT THAT PROVIDES FAIR

3:03:54 WAGES, REASONABLE BENEFITS AND

3:03:56 WORKPLACE PROTECTIONS AND

3:03:58 RESPECT ON THE JOB.

3:04:00 SUPPORTING WORKERS TO PROVIDE

3:04:01 WHO PROVIDE THIS LEVEL OF CARE

3:04:03 IS SUPPORTING THE INDIVIDUALS

3:04:05 AND FAMILIES WHO RELY ON THEM

3:04:07 EVERY SINGLE DAY.

3:04:08 A FAIR CONTRACT WILL HELP

3:04:10 IMPROVE EMPLOYEE RETENTION,

3:04:12 STRENGTHEN THE WORKFORCE AND

3:04:14 ENSURE CONTINUITY OF CARE FOR

3:04:16 SOME OF THE COMMONWEALTH'S MOST

3:04:17 VULNERABLE RESIDENTS.

3:04:19 BECAUSE THIS PROGRAM IS LARGELY

3:04:21 PUBLICLY FUNDED, NEGOTIATIONS

3:04:23 MUST CONTINUE IN GOOD FAITH AND

3:04:25 MOVE TOWARD A FAIR AGREEMENT

3:04:27 THAT RECOGNIZES THE VALUE

3:04:29 OF THESE ESSENTIAL WORKERS AND

3:40:53 MAINTAINING AND HEARING THE

3:41:05 THANK YOU, MADAM CHAIR.

3:41:09 PROPOSITION TWO AND A HALF IS A

3:41:11 LAW PASSED BY VOTERS IN 1980

3:41:13 THAT LIMITS HOW MUCH CITIES AND

3:41:15 TOWNS CAN RAISE PROPERTY TAXES.

3:41:19 THE LAW HAS TWO LIMITS WITHIN

3:41:22 IT THE LEVY CEILING AND THE

3:41:23 LEVY LIMIT.

3:41:24 THE LEVY LIMIT DETERMINES HOW

3:41:26 MUCH A CITY MUNICIPALITY CAN

3:41:28 INCREASE PROPERTY TAX

3:41:29 COLLECTION FOR ONE YEAR TO THE

3:41:31 NEXT.

3:41:32 IT'S DETERMINED BY TAXES

3:41:33 GENERATED FROM NEW GROWTH SUCH

3:41:37 AS NEW RESIDENTIAL COMMERCIAL

3:41:38 DEVELOPMENT OR RENOVATIONS TO

3:41:40 EXISTING PROPERTIES THAT

3:41:43 INCREASE ASSESSED VALUE.

3:41:44 THE LEVY CEILING IS THE

3:41:46 ABSOLUTE MAXIMUM AMOUNT

3:41:47 OF PROPERTY TAXES THAT CAN BE

3:41:49 COLLECTED BY A CITY

3:41:51 MUNICIPALITY, A CITY A TOWN

3:41:53 CAN'T COLLECT MORE THAN 22. 5%

3:41:56 OF THE TOTAL VALUE OF ALL

3:41:58 TAXABLE PROPERTY WITHIN ITS

3:41:59 BORDERS.

3:42:03 BECAUSE MASSACHUSETTS

3:42:05 MUNICIPALITIES RELY HEAVILY ON

3:42:06 PROPERTY TAXES TO FUND LOCAL

3:42:08 GOVERNMENT, PROPOSITION TWO AND

3:42:10 A HALF HAS A SIGNIFICANT IMPACT

3:42:11 ON PUBLIC SCHOOLS, POLICE, FIRE

3:42:13 DEPARTMENTS, PUBLIC WORKS,

3:42:16 LIBRARIES, PARKS, RECREATION,

3:42:18 MUNICIPAL EMPLOYEES SALARIES

3:42:20 BENEFITS THAT WE WERE JUST

3:42:21 DISCUSSING.

3:42:24 WE ALSO KNOW THAT THERE'S BEEN

3:42:25 ONGOING DISCUSSION.

3:42:27 HERE AT.

3:42:28 THE CITY AND THROUGHOUT MANY

3:42:29 CITIES AND TOWNS AND AT THE

3:42:30 STATE HOUSE ABOUT POTENTIALLY

3:42:33 LIFTING PROPOSITION TWO AND A

3:42:35 HALF.

3:42:38 IN OCTOBER 2025 WHEN THEY WERE

3:42:40 CALLS TO THE MASSACHUSETTS

3:42:43 LEGISLATURE TO REPEAL

3:42:44 PROPOSITION TWO AND A HALF.

3:42:46 UM, MANY CITY OFFICIALS SAID IT

3:42:50 SHOULD BE REPEALED.

3:42:53 LIMITING PROPERTY TAX REVENUE

3:42:55 GROWTH MEANS THAT THE LOCAL

3:42:57 GOVERNMENT MUST EXERCISE FISCAL

3:42:59 RESPONSIBILITY, ACCOUNTABILITY

3:43:01 ,TRANSPARENCY THIS PROPOSITION

3:43:05 MAKES TAXES MORE PREDICTABLE

3:43:07 FOR RESIDENTS WHO HAVE ENDURED

3:43:10 CHALLENGING ECONOMIC TIMES IN

3:43:11 BOSTON DUE TO SEVERAL YEARS

3:43:13 OF STEEP INCREASES IN THE

3:43:15 PROPERTY TAXES FOR MANY

3:43:17 RESIDENTS OVER THE LAST OVER

3:43:18 THE LAST TWO YEARS THEY'VE SEEN

3:43:22 A DOUBLE DIGIT PROPERTY TAX

3:43:23 INCREASE.

3:43:28 THIS RESOLUTION, I BELIEVE IS

3:43:31 IMPORTANT BECAUSE IT PROVIDES

3:43:33 BOSTON.

3:43:35 IT PROVIDES CITIES AND TOWNS

3:43:37 WITH STRUCTURE OF HOW MUCH THEY

3:43:40 CAN RAISE TAXES BUT ALSO.

3:43:45 LIVE WITHIN THEIR MEANS TO BE

3:43:47 FISCALLY DISCIPLINE, FISCALLY

3:43:48 RESPONSIBLE, TRANSPARENT AND

3:43:50 ACCOUNTABLE.

3:43:53 I BELIEVE WE NEED TO REAFFIRM

3:43:54 OUR COMMITMENT TO THESE

3:43:57 PRINCIPLES AND ENSURE THAT WE

3:44:00 PRACTICE FISCAL DISCIPLINE AND

3:44:03 RESPONSIBILITY AS MUCH AS WE

3:44:05 CAN, KNOWING THAT RESIDENTS ARE

3:44:09 SEEING STEEP PROPERTY TAXES

3:44:13 CONSISTENTLY, IS THERE AN

3:44:16 OPPORTUNITY FOR US TO HAVE A

3:44:19 IMPORTANT DISCUSSION DOWN THE

3:44:20 ROAD ABOUT PROPOSITION TWO AND

3:44:23 A HALF IN TAXES GENERALLY?

3:44:25 I THINK THAT'S ALSO AN

3:48:34 OFFICERS PLAY A CRITICAL ROLE

3:48:36 IN THE CITY OF BOSTON BOSTON

3:48:39 TRANSPORTATION UM TRAFFIC

3:48:41 ENFORCEMENT OFFICERS UM AS WELL

3:48:45 I SEE THEM FREQUENTLY.

3:48:47 THEY DO AN EXCEPTIONAL JOB BUT

3:48:49 THEY'RE OFTEN SOMETIMES THEY'RE

3:48:51 HARASSED AND EVEN PHYSICALLY

3:48:53 ASSAULTED BY THE PUBLIC.

3:48:55 UM WE KNOW AND I MENTIONED

3:48:57 BOSTON POLICE HAS LONG STANDING

3:48:59 ISSUES OF UNDERSTAFFING,

3:49:01 OVERSTRETCHED RESOURCES, FORCED

3:49:03 OVERTIME, LOOMING RETIREMENTS,

3:49:06 BOSTON POLICE OFFICERS WORK 16

3:49:08 HOURS A DAY.

3:49:10 BOSTON POLICE PATROLMEN

3:49:11 ASSOCIATION RECENTLY NOTED THAT

3:49:12 THE DEPARTMENT IS DOWN ROUGHLY

3:49:15 600 OFFICERS OPERATING ROUGHLY

3:49:18 ONE THIRD OF THE WORKFORCE.

3:49:21 THIS AND SIMILAR INCIDENTS

3:49:23 CONTRIBUTE TO LOW MORALE

3:49:25 RETENTION RECRUITMENT.

3:49:28 THE LACK OF SUPPORT OR

3:49:30 INDIFFERENCE WHEN IT COMES TO

3:49:31 SUPPORTING THE POLICE, THE FIRE

3:49:33 EMS HAS A NEGATIVE IMPACT, YOU

3:49:38 KNOW AND ULTIMATELY OUR CITY IS

3:49:40 LESS SAFE BECAUSE OF THAT.

3:49:42 PUBLIC SAFETY BEGINS WITH

3:49:43 SUPPORTING THE FAMILIES OF THE

3:49:45 MEN AND WOMEN WHO PUT

3:49:46 THEMSELVES IN HARM'S WAY EVERY

3:49:47 DAY TO SERVE AND PROTECT OUR

3:49:50 COMMUNITIES.

3:49:51 IT'S CRITICAL THAT WE UH,

3:49:54 ESPECIALLY THE CITY

3:49:55 COUNCIL DENOUNCE ANY VIOLENCE,

3:49:57 ASSAULTS, HARASSMENT AGAINST

3:49:59 THE POLICE CODE ENFORCEMENT

3:50:01 OFFICERS, OFFICIALS AND

3:50:03 TRANSPORTATION OUR DEDICATED

3:50:04 FIRST RESPONDERS AND WE SUPPORT

3:50:07 THEIR FAMILIES AS WELL.

3:50:10 WE ALSO MAKE IT CLEAR THAT

3:50:12 THOSE WHO PUT THEIR LIVES ON

3:50:13 THE LINE TO PROTECT OUR

3:50:15 COMMUNITIES DESERVE OUR

3:50:16 SUPPORT.

3:50:18 WE RESOURCES TO EFFECTIVELY

3:50:19 SERVE THE RESIDENTS OF BOSTON.

3:50:24 TO PARAPHRASE MY GOOD FRIEND

3:50:27 COUNCILOR MICHAEL FLAHERTY, HE

3:50:27 USED TO SAY THAT YES WE HAVE

3:50:29 THE BEST HOSPITALS, COLLEGES,

3:50:31 UNIVERSITIES IN THE COUNTRY

3:50:32 RIGHT HERE IN BOSTON.

3:50:35 WE KNOW THAT THEY ARE THE CROWN

3:50:37 JEWELS OF THE CITY OF BOSTON.

3:50:38 BUT MAKE NO MISTAKE PEOPLE WHO

3:50:40 WANT TO COME HERE AND

3:50:41 EXPERIENCE THEM BECAUSE THEY

3:50:43 BELIEVE IT IS SAFE TO BE HERE

3:50:45 AND THAT IS DUE TO THE WORK

3:50:47 OF OUR DEDICATED FIRST

3:50:48 RESPONDERS.

3:50:51 THEY DON'T GET THE APPRECIATION

3:50:54 OR RESPECT IN MY OPINION FROM

3:50:57 ELECTED OFFICIALS, BUT THEY

3:50:59 CERTAINLY HAVE EARNED IT.

3:51:03 ACKNOWLEDGE THEIR

3:51:05 PROFESSIONALISM, TO ACKNOWLEDGE

3:51:07 THE DANGEROUS POSITION THAT

3:51:09 THEY'RE OFTEN PLACED IN AND TO

3:51:12 LET THEM KNOW THAT THE CITY

3:51:16 COUNCIL HAS THEIR BACK.

3:55:58 IS A FAMILY WHOSE LIFE HAS BEEN

3:56:00 PERMANENTLY CHANGED.

3:56:03 ORGANIZATIONS LIKE MAD MOMS

3:56:05 REMIND US THAT RECOVERY AND

3:56:07 HEALING ARE NOT ONLY INDIVIDUAL

3:56:09 JOURNEYS BUT THEY ARE COMMUNITY

3:56:13 RESPONSIBILITIES THROUGH

3:56:15 ADVOCACY, PEER SUPPORT,

3:56:17 EDUCATION AND CREATING SPACES

3:56:19 WHERE FAMILIES CAN GRIEVE, HEAL

3:56:22 AND SUPPORT ONE ANOTHER.

3:56:24 MAD MOMS HAS BECOME AN

3:56:26 IMPORTANT RESOURCE FOR

3:56:28 INDIVIDUALS AND FAMILIES

3:56:30 NAVIGATING SOME OF LIFE'S MOST

3:56:32 DIFFICULT CHALLENGES.

3:56:34 THIS RESOLUTION ALSO RECOGNIZES

3:56:37 THE EXTRAORDINARY LEADERSHIP

3:56:39 OF JANICE V JOHNSON, WHOSE

3:56:41 DECADES OF SERVICE TO BOSTON

3:56:43 HAVE TOUCHED COUNTLESS LIVES

3:56:45 RATHER THAN ALLOWING PERSONAL

3:56:47 TRAGEDY TO DEFINE HER, SHE

3:56:49 TRANSFORMED IT INTO A MISSION

3:56:51 OF COMPASSION, ENSURING THAT

3:56:53 OTHER FAMILIES HAVE ACCESS TO

3:56:55 THE SUPPORT AND SENSE

3:56:57 OF COMMUNITY THAT EVERY PERSON

3:56:59 DESERVES.

3:56:59 THE OPENING OF THE FIVE STAR

3:57:02 STUDIO CAFE IS ANOTHER EXAMPLE

3:57:04 OF THAT COMMITMENT, CREATING A

3:57:07 SPACE WHERE HEALING, CREATIVITY

3:57:09 AND RECOVERY CAN COME TOGETHER.

4:02:28 SCIENCE IMPROVEMENT GRANT

4:02:30 PROGRAM AWARDS FUNDING STATES

4:02:31 AND UNITS OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT

4:02:33 TO HELP IMPROVE THE QUALITY

4:02:35 OF HOMELESSNESS UH, FORENSIC

4:02:36 SCIENCE AND MEDICAL EXAMINER

4:02:38 SERVICES PROVIDED THE BOSTON

4:02:40 POLICE DEPARTMENT WILL UTILIZE

4:02:41 THE FUNDS REQUESTED THROUGH THE

4:02:44 FY 24 COVER DOE TO OFFSET

4:02:46 TRAINING COSTS ASSOCIATED WITH

4:02:47 THE DEVELOPMENT OF KNOWLEDGE,

4:02:49 SKILLS AND ABILITIES FOR NEWLY

4:02:51 HIRED AND EXISTING STAFF

4:02:52 MEMBERS.

4:02:55 OFFERED FOR THOSE UNDERGOING

4:02:56 CHANGES IN JOB FUNCTIONALITY

4:02:58 DUE TO CHANGES IN WORK

4:03:00 REQUIREMENTS OR THE DEVELOPMENT

4:03:02 OF ADDITIONAL UM EXPERTISE.

4:03:06 UM FIREARMS ANALYSIS UNIT,

4:03:08 IMPRINT UNIT AND CRIME SCENE

4:03:16 ABILITIES AND EXPERIENCES AS A

4:03:18 PROGRAM.
